top200.why: Initiative and queue-collision behavior needed a single deterministic importance authority spanning all ambient producers.
top200.exitCriteria: Added Rust importance engine + priority queue ordering in `core/layer0/ops/src/importance.rs` and `core/layer0/ops/src/attention_queue.rs`; enqueue/status receipts now carry `score`, `band`, `priority`, and `initiative_action`. Added subconscious/client boundary regression guard lane (`client/runtime/systems/ops/subconscious_boundary_guard.ts`) with strict policy + required-check wiring. Remaining exit: complete live runtime validation and cockpit-priority consumption proof.

top200.why: Coordinator routing needs a typed bus contract to reduce manual handoff errors and enable deterministic orchestration scaling.
top200.exitCriteria: Added `core/layer0/swarm_router` crate with typed envelope + queue primitives (`core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs`, `src/main.rs`) implementing `id/reply_to/route/payload/priority/status` and queue schema `swarm_queue_v1`. Remaining exit: cargo lane validation is currently blocked by host dyld build-script stalls (`V6-HOST-BUILD-STALE-001`, `cargo_test_timeout` after stale-reap).

top200.why: Manual IDs and weak run-state tracking increase coordination mistakes under parallel execution pressure.
top200.exitCriteria: Implemented deterministic role-prefixed auto-ID generation and in-flight lifecycle tracker (`pending/in_progress/complete/failed`) in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` (`auto_id`, `InFlightTracker`). Remaining exit: crate test execution blocked by host dyld stall profile (`V6-HOST-BUILD-STALE-001`).

top200.upgrade: Self-healing reroute policy for failed tasks
top200.why: Swarm resiliency requires deterministic retry/reassign behavior rather than manual intervention loops.
top200.exitCriteria: Implemented policy-backed retry/reroute logic (`RecoveryPolicy`, `recovery_decision`) with receipt envelope support in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` + CLI `recover` path in `src/main.rs`. Remaining exit: cargo test validation blocked by host build-script stalls.

top200.why: Parallel throughput is capped when worker scaling remains manual during backlog spikes.
top200.exitCriteria: Implemented deterministic scaling planner (`ScalingPolicy`, `plan_scaling`) with reversible `scale_up/scale_down/hold` decisions in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` and CLI `scale` command. Remaining exit: cargo test validation blocked by host build-script stalls.

top200.why: Reliable worker handoff and scheduling need schema-backed queue artifacts and deterministic priority sorting.
top200.exitCriteria: Implemented file-backed queue artifact (`QueueArtifact`) with schema versioning (`swarm_queue_v1`), deterministic validation, and priority/tie-break ordering (`priority desc`, `id asc`) in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs`. Remaining exit: cargo test validation blocked by host build-script stalls.

top200.why: Router operations need auditable traces and safe upgrade/rollback controls to avoid orchestration drift.
top200.exitCriteria: Added swarm receipt/metric primitives (`SwarmReceipt`, `SwarmMetrics`) and upgrade/rollback protocol (`apply_upgrade`, `apply_rollback`) in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` with CLI surfaces in `src/main.rs`. Remaining exit: cargo test validation blocked by host build-script stalls.

top200.why: The architecture spec existed externally but needed executable repo contracts and Layer2 initiative primitives tied to it.
top200.exitCriteria: Added canonical contract doc `docs/SYSTEM-ARCHITECTURE-SPECS.md`, derived requirement `docs/client/requirements/REQ-28-conscious-subconscious-iceberg-v1.md`, linked `ARCHITECTURE.md` to the contract, and shipped Layer2 deterministic initiative primitives in `core/layer2/execution/src/initiative.rs` (`initiative-score`, `initiative-action`, `attention-priority`). Remaining exit: complete migration of live queue authority from `core/layer0/ops` to Layer2 lanes without regression.

top200.why: Split authority between Rust spine lanes and Node wrappers can reintroduce drift in behavior, codes, and performance under load.
top200.exitCriteria: Define one Rust-authoritative runtime command schema and migrate Node surfaces to thin conduit wrappers only; parity suite proves identical receipt/error contracts across CLI and daemon paths.

top200.why: Current push mode still relies on queue-mutation wake loops and drain polling semantics rather than a true socket stream.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `protheusd subscribe --transport=ws` with native WebSocket stream frames, resumable cursor/ack contract, bounded heartbeat/reconnect logic, and deterministic stream lifecycle receipts (`open`, `message`, `ack`, `reconnect`, `close`).

top200.why: Cadence parity requires maintainers to execute remote tagging/publishing repeatedly over time.
top200.exitCriteria: Publish at least 9 semantic releases with signed notes and artifact checks; blocked on maintainer release authority + calendar execution.

top200.upgrade: Bioethics/consent policy gate contract for biological interaction

top200.exitCriteria: Human-approved ethics/consent policy artifacts are linked (`HMAN-BIO-001`), Layer-1 policy lanes enforce revocable consent + high-risk disable paths, and all bio interactions are fully auditable via deterministic receipts.

top200.why: Quantum broker claims require real provider execution evidence and credentialed access.
top200.exitCriteria: Human-approved provider access is active (`HMAN-084`) and signed live QPU run artifacts satisfy fidelity/latency policy thresholds.

top200.why: Ternary lowering claims need real hardware proof before production promotion.
top200.exitCriteria: Human-approved hardware access (`HMAN-081`) plus benchmark artifact bundle shows class-scoped gains and no safety-policy violations.

top200.why: Neural interface paths require ethics/legal/hardware governance not executable from autonomous lanes.
top200.exitCriteria: Human approvals (`HMAN-082`, `HMAN-083`) and device-access evidence complete staged safety validation before lane promotion.

top200.why: Startup still pays unnecessary identity burn when `SOUL` and memory-protocol context are eagerly loaded.
top200.exitCriteria: Replace eager identity preload with minimal boot identity stub; defer `SOUL`/memory protocol pages until query intent requires them; enforce startup token ceiling with deterministic hydration receipts.

top200.why: Long-running coding sessions suffer instruction fade and control drift when planning and execution share one loop.
top200.exitCriteria: Add Layer-2 dual-agent coordinator contract with planner/executor split and deterministic handoff receipts (`core/layer2/orchestrator/dual_agent.rs`); add Layer-3 persona split template (`client/cognition/personas/dual_agent_template/`); prove shadows run in dual-agent mode with planner re-injection preventing instruction fade-out.

top200.why: Full upfront tool-manifest preload bloats context and increases cold/query burn in coding sessions.
top200.exitCriteria: Add on-demand lazy tool discovery lane (`core/layer2/tools/lazy_discovery.rs`) that loads tools only when planner requests them; all discoveries route via conduit with receipts; verify no full manifest preload at boot and >=70% context-bloat reduction on long tasks.

top200.why: High-token contexts degrade coding reliability unless relevance-ranked compaction runs continuously.
top200.exitCriteria: Add Layer-1 compaction policy + Layer-2 engine (`core/layer1/context/compaction.rs`) to trim/summarize/rank context every N steps or threshold breach; store compacted context as Epistemic Object with provenance; pass recall-accuracy regression suite at 100k+ token context envelope.

top200.why: Instruction adherence decays over long autonomous coding runs without deterministic reminder re-injection.
top200.exitCriteria: Add reminder lane (`core/layer2/reminders/event_reminder.rs`) that injects structured planner reminders on step/time/drift triggers (10-15 steps or 5-minute cadence by policy); every reminder emits deterministic receipt; no manual reminder intervention required in long tasks.

top200.why: Session continuity still depends on ad hoc recovery rather than deterministic persistent state reconstruction.
top200.exitCriteria: Add persistent memory service (`core/layer1/memory/persistent.rs`) that survives daemon restarts/disconnects and reconstructs planner/executor state across `protheus attach`; persist as Epistemic Objects with rollback pointers; pass cross-session recall regression checks.

top200.upgrade: OpenDev strict autonomous safety controls for coding execution

top200.exitCriteria: Add Layer-1 autonomous safety policy lane (`core/layer1/policy/autonomous_safety.rs`) and Layer-0 enforcement bindings so high-risk actions (terminal/file-write/code-run) require capability grant + policy check + receipt; `protheus-top` shows complete audit trail for autonomous actions.

top200.upgrade: Integrity reseal auto-clear lane for trusted update classes (`REQ-37`)

top200.exitCriteria: Add policy-gated auto-reseal path that clears trusted reseal classes with signed receipts; blocked reseals must emit explicit remediation hints and never silently suppress cockpit runtime.

top200.why: Public certification claims require legal approval plus externally issued evidence artifacts.
top200.exitCriteria: Publish compliance posture report with linked third-party artifacts once `HMAN-026` and `HMAN-027` are complete.

top200.why: A+ procurement requires public/legal packet completeness beyond technical controls and cannot be auto-published.
top200.exitCriteria: Legal-approved packet is published and linked, with evidence attached under `state/ops/evidence/`; owner `HMAN-032`.

top200.upgrade: First reference customer case study publication
top200.why: Fortune-100 procurement requires public reference proof and measurable production outcomes.
top200.exitCriteria: At least one legally approved public case study is published with before/after metrics and evidence links; owner `HMAN-033`.

top200.why: Enterprise buying motions frequently require marketplace procurement paths and listing trust signals.
top200.exitCriteria: At least one marketplace listing is published with current release artifacts and governance links; owner `HMAN-034`.

top200.why: Contract-grade SLA commitments require staffed escalation ownership, not template-only documentation.
top200.exitCriteria: Published support roster and escalation contacts are active and validated in drill evidence; owner `HMAN-035`.

top200.why: Production-grade ternary/qubit validation cannot be closed without hardware/provider access evidence.
top200.exitCriteria: Execute live advanced-substrate validation runs and publish signed evidence bundle once target hardware/provider access is available.

top200.why: Final publication (GitHub release, npm publish, public posting) requires maintainer account authority and client/legal/commercial approval outside autonomous execution.
top200.exitCriteria: Human-owned publication evidence is attached via authority artifacts (`HMAN-028`, `HMAN-029`) and corresponding release/post links are recorded in state evidence.

top200.upgrade: Monero network/compliance profile decision for production rollout
top200.why: Production payment rollout requires explicit client/legal/compliance and operational policy approval for Monero usage.
top200.exitCriteria: Publish approved Monero usage policy + rollout profile before enabling production payment lanes.

top200.upgrade: Sandbox runtime substrate decision (container vs lightweight jail)
top200.why: Full sandbox rollout depends on approved runtime substrate across target environments and security policy.
top200.exitCriteria: Approve sandbox substrate strategy and publish policy contract before full implementation rollout.

top200.why: Event-driven auto-reactions and isolated worktree operations need explicit policy approval before rollout.
top200.exitCriteria: Gate `REQ-17-005` behind an approved enable policy and define rollback contract before activation.

top200.why: External assurance requires non-self-asserted verification from respected third parties.
top200.exitCriteria: Human-contracted external verification is completed and published with linked findings/remediation evidence (`HMAN-087`).

top200.why: High-stakes profile claims require governance/legal scope approval beyond technical implementation.
top200.exitCriteria: Human-approved high-assurance profile scope and claim boundaries are published, and profile artifacts pass policy + proof + benchmark gates (`HMAN-086`).

top200.upgrade: Real-time monitoring + intelligent alert context lane
top200.why: Operators need live system/shadow health with auto-attached context to triage issues quickly.
top200.exitCriteria: `protheus-top`/dashboard surfaces live CPU/memory/network/process/agent-health telemetry with alert events that include snapshot/log/metric context and deterministic alert receipts.
top200.section: Self-Hosted Observability + Orchestration + Incident Response Intake (xyOps, 2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Visual workflow editor + job scheduling lane
top200.why: Complex automation requires a first-class workflow/scheduler surface beyond ad hoc command chains.
top200.exitCriteria: Add visual workflow editor + cron/event scheduling so users can run workflows via dashboard/CLI (`protheus orchestrate workflow`) with full receipt-audited execution traces.
top200.section: Self-Hosted Observability + Orchestration + Incident Response Intake (xyOps, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Reliability posture improves when incidents can trigger governed, deterministic auto-response playbooks.
top200.exitCriteria: Incident triggers run policy-bounded response actions (snapshots/log capture/recovery hooks/escalation) with deterministic receipts and explicit escalation path artifacts.
top200.section: Self-Hosted Observability + Orchestration + Incident Response Intake (xyOps, 2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Self-hosted single-command deploy profile for observability stack
top200.why: Adoption requires low-friction self-hosted deployment without vendor lock-in or mandatory telemetry.
top200.exitCriteria: Provide one-command self-hosted deployment profile (Docker-first) for monitoring/workflow/incident stack, with no mandatory external telemetry and deterministic deployment health receipts.
top200.section: Self-Hosted Observability + Orchestration + Incident Response Intake (xyOps, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Monitoring and response operations must remain Safety Plane governed and auditable.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit gates for all monitor/workflow/incident actions; every event/action yields deterministic receipts and bypass attempts fail closed under policy/capability checks.
top200.section: Self-Hosted Observability + Orchestration + Incident Response Intake (xyOps, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Multi-agent agency execution needs explicit reporting lines, titles, and goal ownership to operate as a coherent org rather than ad hoc groups.
top200.exitCriteria: Add company layer model that defines org charts, reporting edges, titles, and team goals; `protheus orchestrate agency <team>` instantiates hierarchy artifacts visible in `protheus-top` and receipted state.
top200.section: Paperclip Company Layer & Zero-Human Orchestration Intake (x.com/hasantoxr 2030604968416412144, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Long-running autonomous teams require hard budget containment to prevent runaway spend/risk.
top200.exitCriteria: Add policy-backed per-agent budget ledgers with period windows and hard-stop enforcement; budget breaches fail closed with deterministic budget receipts and no silent overrun paths.
top200.section: Paperclip Company Layer & Zero-Human Orchestration Intake (x.com/hasantoxr 2030604968416412144, 2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Ticket system with immutable audit chain

top200.upgrade: 24/7 heartbeat agent runtime + remote monitoring surface

top200.why: Company-level orchestration actions must remain strictly within Safety Plane authority boundaries.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit gates for org changes, budget operations, ticket transitions, and heartbeat controls; all actions produce deterministic receipts and fail-closed denials on policy/capability violations.
top200.section: Paperclip Company Layer & Zero-Human Orchestration Intake (x.com/hasantoxr 2030604968416412144, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Orchestration quality and launch speed improve when production-ready role templates include explicit rules, voice, workflows, and success metrics.
top200.exitCriteria: Add role template pack (`frontend-wizard`, `security-engineer`, `growth-hacker`, `reality-checker`, etc.) with one-command shadow creation and deterministic template-activation receipts.

top200.why: Complex projects need predictable multi-role composition across divisions rather than ad hoc role grouping.
top200.exitCriteria: Add division model (engineering/design/marketing/growth/qa/etc.) and orchestration manifests so `protheus orchestrate agency ...` can deploy cross-division teams with deterministic handoff receipts.

top200.why: Parallel specialized-agent execution needs a dedicated coordinator for decomposition, fan-out, and merge decisions under one contract.
top200.exitCriteria: Extend Layer-2 orchestrator with deterministic multi-agent run coordination (5-10 concurrent agents), with coordinator decisions and child-run lineage visible in `protheus-top` receipts.

top200.upgrade: Workflow + success-metric contract binding for agency runs
top200.why: Agency templates are not production-grade without explicit deliverable gates and measurable run outcomes.
top200.exitCriteria: Bind each agent template to workflow stages + metric contracts; orchestrated runs must output deliverable artifacts and success metrics as receipted Epistemic Objects.

top200.upgrade: Conduit-only activation/handoff guardrails for agency mode
top200.why: Multi-agent activation/handoffs must remain fully Safety Plane governed to prevent implicit authority widening.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for all role activations, tool invocations, and inter-agent handoffs; any bypass attempt fails closed with deterministic denial receipts.

top200.why: Distributed RSI seed quality depends on bounded peer discovery, handoff, critique, and breakthrough exchange across nodes.
top200.exitCriteria: Add governed gossip protocol for node discovery + task/insight exchange with deterministic peer/session/handoff receipts; include compatibility bridge contract for approved external agent networks.

top200.upgrade: Compute sharing + social credit ledger lane

top200.exitCriteria: Add compute-offer/accept execution contracts with metered contribution accounting and social-credit ledger updates as Epistemic Objects; credits are auditable and reversible by policy.

top200.why: Compute-share claims require anti-spoof validation before credit or scheduling trust is granted.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement periodic challenge/response verifier (matmul class) with deterministic proof receipts; credit awards are blocked unless proof policy passes.
top200.section: P2P Gossip Network & Compute-Sharing Seed Intake (2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Idle RSS read/commentary collaboration behavior lane

top200.exitCriteria: Add policy-gated idle behavior where detached nodes run RSS ingestion/commentary workflows with budget caps, peer-comment lineage, and deterministic receipts for every idle cycle action.
top200.section: P2P Gossip Network & Compute-Sharing Seed Intake (2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: One-command browser/CLI node onboarding lane (`protheus node join`)

top200.exitCriteria: Add CLI + browser onboarding contracts that register nodes, complete identity/capability handshake, and join gossip mesh with deterministic join receipts and fail-closed policy gates.

top200.upgrade: Conduit-only network safety guardrails + full activity audit lane

top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for all network discovery, gossip exchange, compute-share, idle behavior, and credit mutations; bypass attempts fail closed and all actions emit deterministic audit receipts.

top200.why: Dataset discovery is fragmented across isolated sources and nodes, limiting RSI network utility and repeatability for research ingestion.

top200.upgrade: Secure Data Sharing with Provenance Contracts

top200.exitCriteria: Implement capability/tokenized sharing envelopes where each shared dataset carries Epistemic metadata (provenance/confidence/policy/retention) and produces a deterministic receipt chain; raw transfer denied without explicit grant.

top200.why: Current workflows often require copying data to compute, increasing exposure and policy risk for sensitive datasets.
top200.exitCriteria: Add remote compute-to-data execution so analysis runs at data owner node and returns bounded result artifacts only; raw dataset remains local and policy-enforced receipts prove execution path.

top200.why: Shared datasets are not consistently indexed with lineage relationships, reducing traceability and downstream recall quality.

top200.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Discovery/sharing/compute marketplace actions can bypass canonical audit flow without explicit conduit gating.

top200.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Time-series container primitive for receipts/events/memory logs

top200.exitCriteria: Add a core time-series container contract with time-partitioning + index strategy for receipts, heartbeat, and event lanes; verify high-ingest profile (`100k+/s` target lane benchmark) and recent-window low-latency query receipts.

top200.upgrade: Hybrid ingest/query acceleration lane (memory-hot + durable tier)

top200.exitCriteria: Implement hybrid ingestion/tiering path with hot in-memory write/query acceleration and durable persistence; benchmark/receipt evidence shows material improvement over baseline file/SQLite paths under controlled workload gates.

top200.upgrade: Scale-out sharding/replication contract for network seed nodes
top200.why: Distributed seed operation requires resilient multi-node storage without single-node bottlenecks.
top200.exitCriteria: Add shard/replica coordination contracts for time-series lanes with deterministic consistency and failover receipts; multi-node harness demonstrates no single point of failure under node-loss scenarios.

top200.upgrade: Workload-aware data model selector (time-series vs key-value vs document lanes)

top200.exitCriteria: Add policy-governed model-selection layer that routes workloads to optimal storage model or hybrid mode with deterministic selection rationale in receipts.

top200.upgrade: Conduit-only storage operations guardrail + audit lane

top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for all storage writes/queries/compaction/tiering actions; every operation emits deterministic receipts and bypass attempts fail closed.

top200.why: Reply handling and meeting booking are fragmented and can miss qualified responses in high-volume campaigns.

top200.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Campaign execution stalls without continuous schedule/heartbeat operation when users are offline.
top200.exitCriteria: Support persistent scheduled/background operation with campaign state checkpoints, health markers, and resumable execution receipts.

top200.why: Outreach apps can bypass policy controls or drift into client-owned authority paths without explicit placement and boundary contracts.

top200.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Persona/shadow integration for WiFi sensing as native Eye

top200.upgrade: Biological substrate interface lane (living-neuron signal -> model control)
top200.why: Layer `-1` needs a formal biological adapter contract for real-time neuron-signal modulation of model behavior.
top200.exitCriteria: Add interface contract for multi-electrode neuronal input streams that can deterministically map spike activity to bounded model-control parameters (temperature/sampling/attention controls) with per-event receipts.

top200.why: Bidirectional neuron-AI coupling requires explicit feedback/stimulation control with safe fallback behavior.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement closed-loop lane where model state can issue bounded stimulation patterns; add deterministic degrade path to silicon-only mode on signal loss or health-policy failure.

top200.why: Biological computing must fit the existing exotic-hardware substrate template without leaking substrate specifics into Layer 0.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `layer_minus_one` biological adapter template declaring capabilities (spike-rate channels, stimulation channels, health telemetry), with Layer-0 remaining substrate-agnostic through conduit envelopes.

top200.upgrade: CLI/persona observability integration for biological substrate
top200.why: Advanced users need explicit enablement/monitoring surfaces to safely use biological substrate adapters.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `protheus substrate enable biological` + monitoring surfaces in `protheus-top`/dashboard, and persona hooks for research/health workflows with policy-scoped activation receipts.

top200.upgrade: WebGPU + WGSL accelerated browser inference runtime
top200.why: Zero-cloud local inference at high throughput requires a dedicated browser substrate contract rather than generic model paths.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `layer_minus_one/webgpu` adapter profile supporting quantized inference (INT4/INT8/FP16), WGSL kernel path, and SafeTensors ingestion with benchmark gate targeting >=150 tok/s on supported class hardware.

top200.why: Privacy-first/browser portability requires full local execution without mandatory external API/server dependencies.
top200.exitCriteria: Prove browser-local inference sessions run end-to-end in one tab with no required external inference endpoints and deterministic local-only mode receipts.

top200.upgrade: Model-agnostic SafeTensors loader + adaptive quantization selector
top200.why: Practical adoption requires multi-model loading with automatic quantization/memory fit decisions.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement loader contract for arbitrary SafeTensors models with policy-bounded quantization selection based on device memory/runtime constraints and deterministic loader decision receipts.

top200.upgrade: Conduit-routed browser inference guardrails + token-stream receipts
top200.why: High-speed browser inference must remain Safety Plane governed despite client-local execution paths.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only invocation and deterministic token-stream receipt logging for browser inference calls; bypass attempts fail closed with policy denial receipts.

top200.upgrade: Persona/shadow substrate switch contract (`protheus substrate enable webgpu`)
top200.why: Operators need explicit routing controls to use browser-native inference for privacy-sensitive or edge-constrained tasks.
top200.exitCriteria: Add substrate switch/selection contract and thin client wrappers so shadows/personas can select `webgpu` profile under policy/budget gates with observability metrics (tokens/s, latency, fallback reason) in receipts.

top200.why: Agent runtime needs deterministic self-profile generation (model/runtime/GPU/flags) for better autonomous diagnostics and reproducible identity docs.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `protheus shadow discover` lane producing signed identity artifact (runtime profile + capability surface + model/runtime metadata) with conduit receipts and replay-safe provenance.

top200.upgrade: Premium real-time TUI envelope for shadow/cockpit execution
top200.why: Operator ergonomics and observability are limited without a high-signal live execution surface.
top200.exitCriteria: Upgrade `protheus-top`/watch surfaces with rich real-time stream blocks (timings, tool call classes, status colors, optional ASCII header skins) while preserving deterministic machine-readable telemetry contracts.

top200.upgrade: Persistent memory continuity hardening across attach/disconnect boundaries
top200.why: Persistent cockpit exists, but continuity quality still needs explicit cross-session reconstruction guarantees for user modeling + learned context.
top200.exitCriteria: Add continuity contract tests proving context/user model reconstruction across daemon restart and detached/reattached sessions with deterministic reconstruction receipts.

top200.upgrade: Subagent delegation primitives + governed tool-rich execution packs
top200.why: Autonomous workflows need first-class delegated execution packs under strict capability controls.
top200.exitCriteria: Add subagent delegation primitives with policy-scoped tool packs (file/terminal/browser/code/cron) and deterministic parent-child receipt chains for each delegated run.

top200.why: New autonomous affordances must remain fail-closed under Safety Plane authority.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 policy checks for self-discovery, tool execution, and delegation actions; any bypass attempt fails closed and emits auditable denial receipts.

top200.upgrade: Sandboxed virtual browser runtime with low-latency streaming
top200.why: Research and operator flows need isolated browser/desktop sessions without exposing host browser state.
top200.exitCriteria: Add containerized browser runtime (ephemeral + persistent profiles) with low-latency stream transport and strict host-state isolation guarantees validated by policy receipts.

top200.why: Collaborative shadow/user investigation requires shared sessions with explicit control boundaries.
top200.exitCriteria: Support joined session roles (`watch-only`, `shared-control`) with deterministic control handoff, participant actions, and session-event receipts.

top200.why: Browser automation requires both scriptability and real-time operator observability.
top200.exitCriteria: Provide governed automation execution inside session container (Playwright/Puppeteer-style) with live telemetry surfaced to `protheus-top` and auditable run receipts.

top200.upgrade: Privacy/security controls for streamed browser sessions
top200.why: Streaming sessions can widen exfiltration/risk without strict privacy/network/budget controls.
top200.exitCriteria: Add policy-gated controls for network posture (e.g., tor/vpn profiles), session recording/broadcast toggles, and hard budgets (duration/data/request volume) with fail-closed enforcement receipts.

top200.upgrade: InfRing-native CLI + observability integration for streamed sessions
top200.why: Adoption depends on first-class commands and consistent observability surfaces.
top200.exitCriteria: Add thin wrappers for `protheus browser` / `protheus shadow --browser` with conduit-only execution and dashboard stream integration; every session action emits deterministic receipts under persona/policy governance.

top200.why: The architecture needs one canonical primitive surface before additional profile-specific work can be trusted.
top200.exitCriteria: Add versioned primitive contract registry (`planes/contracts/metakernel_primitives_v1.json`) and enforce no-unknown-primitive usage in lane/config validation checks.

top200.upgrade: Invariant gate for the first eight metakernel invariants
top200.why: Safety guarantees must be executable and fail-closed, not descriptive text only.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `ops:metakernel:invariants` lane that evaluates all eight invariants with deterministic pass/fail receipts and CI required-check wiring.

top200.why: Portable deploy units need a strict manifest contract for capability/budget/provenance correctness.
top200.exitCriteria: Ship `cellbundle.schema.json` + `protheus-ops metakernel-manifest validate` with strict mode and deterministic error receipts for invalid bundles.

top200.upgrade: WIT world registry + wasm component ABI contract
top200.why: Pluggable substrate execution requires a stable component boundary independent of TS wrappers.
top200.exitCriteria: Add WIT world registry under `planes/contracts/wit/`, validate declared worlds from bundle manifests, and run compatibility checks in CI.

top200.upgrade: Effect-typed capability taxonomy + risk class policy defaults (R0-R4)
top200.why: Capability safety depends on typed effects and deterministic risk-tier handling rules.
top200.exitCriteria: Add capability taxonomy policy (`observe/infer/store/communicate/actuate/train/quantum/admin`) with R0-R4 defaults; gate high-risk effects via policy checks and receipts.

top200.why: Budgets must bind before execution to prevent post-hoc overrun behavior.
top200.exitCriteria: Add admission-control lane that denies execution when any hard budget is exceeded and records bounded reason codes in receipts.

top200.why: AI-native state needs typed uncertainty and lineage metadata, not byte-only storage semantics.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `epistemic_object_v1` schema + validators and require all durable memory objects to include lineage + confidence fields.

top200.why: External side effects must be journaled deterministically to preserve replay and audit guarantees.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce no unjournaled external effect path in runtime gates; actuation calls fail-closed without associated journal entries.

top200.why: Portable execution across MCU/edge/cloud/quantum/neural nodes requires machine-readable substrate contracts.
top200.exitCriteria: Add substrate descriptor registry with required fields (determinism, latency, energy, isolation, observability, privacy locality) and degrade-path validation.

top200.upgrade: Radix policy guard (binary baseline, ternary optional class-scoped lowering)
top200.why: Radix experimentation must not weaken core security invariants.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce binary-only for crypto/policy/capability/attestation/journal paths; allow ternary lowering only for approved workload classes with receipts.

top200.why: Quantum execution is hybrid and queue-bound; it needs explicit broker semantics and fallback guarantees.
top200.exitCriteria: Add broker API + policy gates (cost/fidelity/latency thresholds) and classical fallback receipts for unavailable/low-fidelity QPU paths.

top200.upgrade: Neural consent kernel scaffold with separated authorities (`observe/infer/feedback/stimulate`)
top200.why: Neural interfaces require strict authority boundaries and highest-risk controls for stimulation paths.
top200.exitCriteria: Add consent-kernel policy surface with dual-control + immutable audit + rate limits for `actuate.neural.stimulate`; fail-closed by default when consent token is absent.

top200.upgrade: Attestation graph service for code/model/policy/data/effect lineage
top200.why: Runtime trust requires signed lineage across artifacts, not isolated per-lane proofs.
top200.exitCriteria: Add attestation graph writer/query lane linking SBOM/signature/transparency-log/measured-boot pointers to execution receipts.

top200.why: Degraded operation must preserve safety while staying operational within declared fallbacks.
top200.exitCriteria: Add verifier ensuring each critical lane declares fallback behavior and that fallback never widens privilege; emit deterministic degraded-mode receipts.

top200.why: Profile claims need reproducible execution evidence, not architecture-only assertions.
top200.exitCriteria: Add profile harness that runs deterministic conformance checks per profile with pass/fail artifacts and regression deltas.

top200.upgrade: Port adaptation primitives to core and demote client runtime
top200.why: Adaptation primitives are currently bootstrapped in TS client runtime for execution speed; authority must live in core to satisfy strict layering/security.
top200.exitCriteria: Port `REQ-19-001`/`002`/`003` primitives to Rust `core/layer2`, expose only conduit APIs, keep `client` Layer 3 as thin compatibility shell + tests, and remove policy authority from client runtime code.

top200.why: Security research workflows need first-class binary and firmware inspection instead of ad hoc external tooling.
top200.exitCriteria: Add scan support for binaries, UEFI images, BA2 archives, and Binary Ninja DB artifacts with rulepack-based detection; store findings with deterministic provenance receipts.

top200.upgrade: MCP analysis server surface for AI-assisted vuln hunting
top200.why: Shadows/personas need delegated binary analysis through governed tool surfaces.
top200.exitCriteria: Expose binary analysis via MCP-compatible service contract (stdio/HTTP-SSE variants) so agents can request scans and receive structured Epistemic Objects with receipt links.

top200.why: Vulnerability workflows require machine-readable outputs with policy context for triage automation.
top200.exitCriteria: Emit structured findings (`json` and streaming `jsonl`) carrying provenance hash, confidence score, policy labels, and deterministic scan metadata for replay/audit.

top200.why: Binary analysis must not bypass Layer-0 guardrails even when processing untrusted artifacts.
top200.exitCriteria: Run all scans through conduit-routed sandbox/container lanes with budget/privacy/degrade policy checks; fail closed on policy violations and persist deterministic receipts for each run.

top200.why: Capability growth requires user-extensible detection rules without modifying authoritative core logic.
top200.exitCriteria: Add client-facing rulepack install/enable contracts with schema validation and signature/provenance checks; user rulepacks can be activated without core source edits.

top200.upgrade: Developer DX + CLI/observability integration for vuln scans
top200.why: Adoption depends on simple entrypoints and visibility into scan outcomes.
top200.exitCriteria: Add thin wrappers for `protheus scan binary ...` and `protheus research --firmware`, plus observability surfaces for scan status/findings in `protheus-top` and dashboard lanes.

top200.why: Scaled web-capable shadows need bounded multi-instance control and immediate operator visibility.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-20-003` multi-instance lifecycle controls, policy caps, and dashboard metrics with receipts.

top200.why: Real-world browser workflows need anti-bot resilience and safe human-assisted checkpoints (e.g., 2FA).
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-20-004` stealth profile + headed handoff transitions under explicit allow/deny policy receipts.

top200.why: Session persistence should be morphable/portable while remaining tamper-evident and fail-closed.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-20-005` encrypted blob-backed continuity with restore/morph integrity checks.

top200.why: Browser coverage and resilience improve when Chrome-first control has policy-driven fallback providers.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-20-006` Firefox/Edge fallback profile contract with typed unsupported-provider failures.

top200.upgrade: Shadow/persona browser governance + drift breaker lane
top200.why: Web-capable autonomy must remain sovereign with drift controls and review escalation on risky actions.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-20-007` shadow/persona bridge, drift telemetry thresholds, breaker hooks, and escalation receipts.

top200.why: Pinch-class parity needs deterministic endpoint conformance checks, not one-off manual demos.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-21-001` core endpoint conformance suite with replayable pass/fail receipts.

top200.why: Browser agent cost control requires hard efficiency ceilings on text and diff payloads.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-21-002` token-budget SLO gates with fail-closed thresholds and benchmark receipts.

top200.why: Operational parity needs measurable startup/client/memory/size budgets across release artifacts.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-21-003` startup latency, RSS, and binary footprint verification with regression deltas.

top200.why: Browser control should remain framework/language-agnostic for broad agent ecosystem adoption.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-21-004` curl-first fixtures and non-TS integration tests preserving receipt/policy metadata.

top200.why: Competitive claims require reproducible parity benchmarks tied to receipts and release evidence.
top200.exitCriteria: Implement `REQ-21-005` measured benchmark matrix publication with dashboard/release integration.

top200.upgrade: Visual team dashboard lane for multi-agent collaboration
top200.why: Team orchestration visibility remains fragmented without a unified real-time collaboration surface.
top200.exitCriteria: Add a live team dashboard (`protheus-top` / `protheus team dashboard`) showing per-agent status, active task, handoff history, and contribution logs with sub-2s refresh and receipt drilldown links.
top200.section: Visual Agent Team Dashboard & Role Templates Intake (Doc `1Aegff87diqP1piog54qjAmWyPrxLOMO4CCoAuOWfmlU`, 2026-03-09)

top200.upgrade: Instant role template launcher with runtime enforcement
top200.why: Fast role-based onboarding needs one-command templates with policy-safe defaults.
top200.exitCriteria: Add role template launcher for common roles (`frontend-engineer`, `qa-tester`, `marketing-strategist`, `research-lead`) so `protheus shadow create --role ...` provisions tool/memory/workflow defaults with deterministic activation receipts.
top200.section: Visual Agent Team Dashboard & Role Templates Intake (Doc `1Aegff87diqP1piog54qjAmWyPrxLOMO4CCoAuOWfmlU`, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Team workflows need built-in scheduling + handoff orchestration under one contract.
top200.exitCriteria: Add scheduling commands for individual shadows and team runs (`protheus schedule ...`) with cron-like triggers, deterministic kickoff/handoff receipts, and automatic summary artifact generation.
top200.section: Visual Agent Team Dashboard & Role Templates Intake (Doc `1Aegff87diqP1piog54qjAmWyPrxLOMO4CCoAuOWfmlU`, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Dashboard actions, role activation, and scheduled runs must stay Safety Plane governed.
top200.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for all team-dashboard controls and schedule triggers; every action emits deterministic receipts and fail-closed denials on policy violations.
top200.section: Visual Agent Team Dashboard & Role Templates Intake (Doc `1Aegff87diqP1piog54qjAmWyPrxLOMO4CCoAuOWfmlU`, 2026-03-09)

top200.why: Multi-channel release packaging is fragmented, limiting adoption and credibility across Rust/TS/Python ecosystems.
top200.exitCriteria: Ship versioned release contract for GitHub Releases + crates.io + npm + PyPI with signed multi-arch artifacts and SBOM bundle; release gate fails if any channel or signature artifact is missing.

top200.why: Existing Python wrapper surface is too thin to compete with first-class Python agent frameworks.
top200.exitCriteria: Add Rust-backed Python SDK under `sdk/python` with async client parity for core APIs (`lens`, `orchestrate`, `shadow`, `research`), receipt propagation, and parity regression tests against TS client behavior.

top200.upgrade: Public benchmark crown harness + reproducibility gate
top200.why: Performance claims are not yet continuously published through one reproducible benchmark lane.
top200.exitCriteria: Add benchmark workflow + artifact pack that runs concurrency/cold-start/throughput/token-efficiency suites in reproducible containers, publishes machine-readable reports, and fails on regressions against policy thresholds.

top200.why: New-user onboarding requires too many manual steps across languages and safety primitives.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `protheus init --lang <python

top200.upgrade: Tool registry + policy-gated marketplace ingestion lane
top200.why: Ecosystem growth is constrained without one governed package/tool ingestion path.
top200.exitCriteria: Add registry + install contract (`protheus hub install`) with constitution/policy checks, signed package metadata, tool manifest schema validation, and deterministic install receipts.

top200.why: Competitors win on simple orchestration semantics while Protheus orchestration surfaces remain fragmented.
top200.exitCriteria: Add declarative orchestration entrypoints (`crew`, `debate`, persistent shadow scheduling) that compile through Safety Plane graphs with typed receipts and conflict-resolution tests.

top200.why: Protocol-war interoperability is incomplete without first-class MCP/A2A support in the core runtime boundary.
top200.exitCriteria: Add native MCP server surface and A2A session bridge through conduit with signed identity/session receipts, conformance tests, and no-bypass three-plane boundary enforcement.

top200.why: Browser-native agent execution path is missing, limiting zero-install reach and standards alignment.
top200.exitCriteria: Add substrate adapter under `planes/substrate/webmcp/` with policy-gated browser session contracts, receipt chaining, and fallback-safe capability restrictions.

top200.upgrade: Agent identity and trust control plane
top200.why: Cross-agent authentication/authorization remains under-specified for standards-aligned deployments.
top200.exitCriteria: Add identity plane contracts for agent IDs, signed session assertions, and trust-chain verification tied to Merkle receipts and governance policy; include replay/revocation tests.

top200.why: Helm/Terraform exist, but there is no first-class operator for autonomous lifecycle management at enterprise scale.
top200.exitCriteria: Add `protheus-operator` packaging and CRDs with lifecycle reconciliation, autoscaling, and receipt export hooks; conformance suite validates install/upgrade/rollback behavior across Kubernetes profiles.

top200.why: Three-plane/conduit interfaces are documented but not published as one machine-readable external compatibility contract.
top200.exitCriteria: Publish versioned Open Agent Contracts spec (schemas + examples + compatibility tests) covering three planes, conduit envelopes, identity/session primitives, and backward-compatibility policy checks.
